vulnerability.title: Configure the Use of the pam_faillock.so Module in the /etc/pam.d/password-auth File.
vulnerability.description: The pam_faillock.so module must be loaded in preauth in /etc/pam.d/password-auth.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if [ -f /usr/bin/authselect ]; then if ! authselect check; then echo " authselect integrity check failed. Remediation aborted! This remediation could not be applied because an authselect profile was not selected or the selected profile is not intact. It is not recommended to manually edit the PAM files when authselect tool is available. In cases where the default authselect profile does not cover a specific demand, a custom authselect profile is recommended." exit 1 fi authselect enable-feature with-faillock authselect apply-changes -b else AUTH_FILES=("/etc/pam.d/system-auth" "/etc/pam.d/password-auth") for pam_file in "${AUTH_FILES[@]}" do if ! grep -qE '^\s*auth\s+required\s+pam_faillock\.so\s+(pre

triage_decision.reason: Enabling pam_faillock.so in the preauth section of password-auth implements account lockout after failed authentication attempts, a standard STIG hardening measure that does not require system reboot and poses minimal risk when default parameters are used.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential lockout if pam_faillock.so parameters are misconfigured (e.g., too low unlock time); Failure to maintain authselect integrity could cause PAM misconfiguration

execution_details.stderr: bash: line 1: [x /usr/bin/authselect ]]: No such file or directory

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: Plan correctly ensures pam_faillock.so is loaded in preauth of /etc/pam.d/password-auth via authselect or manual edit, satisfying the vulnerability requirement.
concerns: Ambiguity in step 5c regarding where to insert the line may lead to incorrect placement if not clearly defined.
suggested_improvements: Insert the pam_faillock.so preauth line before any non-required auth lines to maintain proper ordering.
Check for and avoid duplicate pam_faillock.so entries when editing the file.
If a custom authselect profile is needed, use authselect create-profile to preserve changes.
